questions.question: Choose the correct verb: 'There _______ plenty of job opportunities for the people of this region.'
questions.explanation: After an introductory 'there', the verb must agree with the real subject following it ('opportunities'), which is plural.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: 'There _______ plenty of room for improvement in her work.'
questions.explanation: The real subject 'room' is uncountable and singular, requiring a singular verb.

questions.question: Which verb is correct? 'Two plus two _______ four.'
questions.explanation: Mathematical expressions of addition are typically treated as singular.

questions.question: Correct the verb: 'Two twos _______ four.'
questions.explanation: When multiplication is expressed this way in the textbook activity, it is treated as plural.

questions.question: Select the right form: 'Seventy kilometres _______ a long distance.'
questions.explanation: Plural numbers representing a single figure of distance take a singular verb.

questions.question: Identify the correct verb: 'Fish and chips _______ not so expensive as chicken curry and rice.'
questions.explanation: Two nouns joined by 'and' that represent a single meal or notion take a singular verb.

questions.question: Fill the blank: 'A crowd _______ gathered in the street.'
questions.explanation: A collective noun treated as a singular unit takes a singular verb.

questions.question: Choose the correct form: 'The assembly _______ divided on this issue.'
questions.explanation: When the members of a collective noun are considered as individuals (often indicated by the word 'divided'), a plural verb is used.

questions.question: Correct usage: 'The police _______ chasing the burglar.'
questions.explanation: The word 'police' is always treated as plural and takes a plural verb.

questions.question: Complete the sentence: 'Her clothing _______ waterproof.'
questions.explanation: Nouns like 'clothing', 'furniture', and 'stationery' are uncountable and take singular verbs.

questions.question: Identify the auxiliary: 'Either of the boys _______ won a medal.'
questions.explanation: When a subject begins with 'Either of' followed by a plural noun, the verb is singular.

questions.question: Fill the blank: 'Neither of those officers _______ efficient.'
questions.explanation: 'Neither of' followed by a plural noun requires a singular verb.

questions.question: Verb form: 'One of his friends _______ able to speak English fluently.'
questions.explanation: 'One of' followed by a plural noun takes a singular verb.

questions.question: Correct the verb: 'Some honey _______ left in the pot.'
questions.explanation: When 'some' is followed by a singular/uncountable noun, it takes a singular verb.

questions.question: Agreement Check: 'The governor and chancellor of the university _______ been invited to this function.'
questions.explanation: The lack of the article 'the' before 'chancellor' indicates that both titles refer to the same person, requiring a singular verb.

questions.question: Fraction rule: 'Three-fourths of the crop _______ damaged by the flood.'
questions.explanation: With fractions, the verb agrees with the noun following the fraction ('crop' is singular).

questions.question: Correct the auxiliary: 'A great deal of money _______ spent for his mother’s treatment.'
questions.explanation: The phrase 'a great deal of' is used with uncountable nouns like 'money', requiring a singular verb.

questions.question: Identify the verb: 'A whole regiment of volunteers _______ working here.'
questions.explanation: Phrases like 'a regiment of' followed by a plural noun usually take a singular verb.

questions.question: Rule distinction: 'The number of scholarships _______ increased this year.'
questions.explanation: 'The number of' takes a singular verb because the real subject is 'the number'.

questions.question: Disease rule: 'These days mumps _______ not at all difficult to cure.'
questions.explanation: Names of certain diseases like 'mumps' or 'measles' take singular verbs despite ending in 's'.

questions.question: Clause as subject: 'What he says about me _______ not affect me.'
questions.explanation: If the subject of a sentence is a clause, the verb is singular.

questions.question: Connective rule: 'Ram as well as his friends _______ to play football.'
questions.explanation: With 'as well as', the verb agrees with the first subject ('Ram'), which is singular.

questions.question: Identify the verb: 'Neither rose nor jasmine _______ available in the market.'
questions.explanation: With 'neither...nor', the verb agrees with the closer subject ('jasmine'), which is singular.

questions.question: Pair noun rule: 'A pair of spectacles _______ lying on the bed.'
questions.explanation: While 'spectacles' is plural, 'a pair of spectacles' is singular.

questions.question: Adjective rule: 'The rich _______ in affluence.'
questions.explanation: When an adjective like 'rich' is used with 'the' to represent a class, it takes a plural verb.

questions.question: Title rule: 'The Canterbury Tales _______ written by Chaucer.'
questions.explanation: A plural proper name of a single book or work requires a singular verb.

questions.question: Time rule: 'Six weeks _______ a long vacation for students.'
questions.explanation: A plural noun expressing a period of time as a single unit takes a singular verb.

questions.question: Identify the correct verb: 'Your trousers _______ torn.'
questions.explanation: Nouns like 'trousers' and 'scissors' are treated as plural.

questions.question: Question tag rule: 'I am young and strong, _______ ?'
questions.explanation: The textbook identifies 'aren't I' as the correct tag for 'I am'.

questions.question: Relative clause rule: 'It is you who _______ insulted me.'
questions.explanation: The verb in a relative clause agrees with the antecedent ('you').

questions.question: Check introductory 'there': 'There _______ people waiting to see you.'
questions.explanation: Verb agrees with 'people' (plural).

questions.question: Identify the tag: 'Ours is a great nation, _______ ?'
questions.explanation: The subject 'nation' is singular, requiring 'isn't it'.

questions.question: Identify the verb: 'The police _______ a difficult job.'
questions.explanation: 'Police' always takes a plural verb.

questions.question: Correct usage: 'A lot of cattle _______ grazing in the field.'
questions.explanation: 'Cattle' is a plural noun, requiring 'were'.

questions.question: Identify the verb: 'The youth _______ not so sincere (general class).'
questions.explanation: When 'the youth' refers to young people in general, it is plural.

questions.question: Verb agreement: 'The youth _______ more serious than his uncle (specific person).'
questions.explanation: When referring to a specific young man, 'youth' is singular.

questions.question: Find the verb: 'Ill news _______ fast.'
questions.explanation: 'News' is uncountable and takes a singular verb.

questions.question: Disease agreement: 'Mumps _______ very unpleasant.'
questions.explanation: 'Mumps' is a singular subject.

questions.question: Science rule: 'Bacteria of the harmful kind _______ diseases.'
questions.explanation: 'Bacteria' is the plural form of 'bacterium', requiring a plural verb.

questions.question: Introductory rule: 'There _______ a pair of spectacles on that shelf.'
questions.explanation: Agreeing with 'a pair' (singular).

questions.question: Plural rule: 'My brothers _______ here.'
questions.explanation: Basic subject-verb agreement with a plural subject.

questions.question: Weight rule: 'Ten kilos _______ a heavy load.'
questions.explanation: Weights representing a single quantity are singular.

questions.question: Time unit: 'Five minutes _______ enough to finish.'
questions.explanation: Periods of time treated as a single unit take a singular verb.

questions.question: Money rule: 'One hundred dollars _______ too much for this pen.'
questions.explanation: Amounts of money treated as a single sum take a singular verb.

questions.question: Single notion: 'Bread and jam _______ my favorite snack.'
questions.explanation: Two nouns suggesting a single idea take a singular verb.

questions.question: Collective unit: 'The class _______ very noisy today.'
questions.explanation: Collective noun acting as a single unit.

questions.question: Collective individuals: 'The crew _______ arguing about their duties.'
questions.explanation: When members of a group act individually (indicated by 'arguing' or 'their duties'), use a plural verb.

questions.question: Identify the verb: 'People _______ unhappy with the new tax.'
questions.explanation: 'People' is a plural noun.

questions.question: Furniture check: 'The furniture _______ delivered yesterday.'
questions.explanation: 'Furniture' is uncountable and singular.

questions.question: Either of rule: 'Either of the candidates _______ suitable.'
questions.explanation: 'Either of' always takes a singular verb.

questions.question: Neither of rule: 'Neither of my parents _______ home.'
questions.explanation: 'Neither of' takes a singular verb.

questions.question: None of rule: 'None of the students _______ completed the task.'
questions.explanation: The textbook identifies 'none of' as taking a singular verb.

questions.question: Each of rule: 'Each of the flowers _______ beautiful.'
questions.explanation: 'Each of' takes a singular verb.

questions.question: One of rule: 'One of the eggs _______ broken.'
questions.explanation: Verb agrees with 'one'.

questions.question: Fraction rule (singular): 'One-third of the city _______ flooded.'
questions.explanation: Verb agrees with 'city' (singular).

questions.question: Fraction rule (plural): 'Half of the boys _______ absent.'
questions.explanation: Verb agrees with 'boys' (plural).

questions.question: Phrase rule: 'A lot of books _______ missing.'
questions.explanation: Verb agrees with 'books'.

questions.question: Phrase rule: 'A lot of work _______ left.'
questions.explanation: Verb agrees with 'work' (uncountable).

questions.question: Collective phrase: 'A crowd of fans _______ waiting.'
questions.explanation: 'A crowd of' usually takes a singular verb.

questions.question: Phrase rule: 'A number of mistakes _______ made.'
questions.explanation: 'A number of' takes a plural verb.

questions.question: Phrase rule: 'The number of cases _______ rising.'
questions.explanation: 'The number of' takes a singular verb.

questions.question: Subject rule: 'Physics _______ my favorite subject.'
questions.explanation: 'Physics' is singular.

questions.question: Identify the verb: 'What he does _______ none of your business.'
questions.explanation: A clause as a subject is singular.

questions.question: Agreement rule: 'The captain as well as the players _______ cheering.'
questions.explanation: With 'as well as', agree with the first subject.

questions.question: Agreement rule: 'The teacher, together with her students, _______ going on a field trip.'
questions.explanation: With 'together with', agree with the first subject.

questions.question: Correlative rule: 'Not only the teacher but also the students _______ happy.'
questions.explanation: With 'not only...but also', agree with the nearer subject ('students').

questions.question: Correlative rule: 'Either my brothers or my sister _______ cleaning the house.'
questions.explanation: Agree with nearer subject ('sister').

questions.question: Correlative rule: 'Neither the boys nor the girls _______ arrived.'
questions.explanation: Agree with nearer subject ('girls').

questions.question: Agreement check: 'My glasses _______ on the table.'
questions.explanation: 'Glasses' is a plural noun.

questions.question: Identify the verb: 'A pair of trousers _______ required.'
questions.explanation: 'A pair of...' is singular.

questions.question: Identify the verb: 'The blind _______ help crossing the road.'
questions.explanation: 'The blind' refers to a plural class.

questions.question: Agreement check: 'Gulliver's Travels _______ a famous book.'
questions.explanation: Titles of books are singular.

questions.question: Identify the verb: 'It is I who _______ responsible.'
questions.explanation: In relative clauses, the verb agrees with the antecedent ('I').

questions.question: Relative check: 'The boys who _______ playing are my friends.'
questions.explanation: Antecedent 'boys' is plural.

questions.question: Identify the verb: 'The poet and singer _______ here (same person).'
questions.explanation: One person, two titles (singular).

questions.question: Identify the verb: 'The poet and the singer _______ here (two people).'
questions.explanation: Two different people (indicated by 'the' before each).

questions.question: Agreement check: 'No student _______ present.'
questions.explanation: 'No' + singular noun takes a singular verb.

questions.question: Agreement check: 'No students _______ present.'
questions.explanation: 'No' + plural noun takes a plural verb.

questions.question: Subject check: 'Mathematics _______ difficult for some.'
questions.explanation: Singular subject.

questions.question: Agreement Check: 'Honesty _______ the best policy.'
questions.explanation: Abstract nouns like 'Honesty' take a singular verb.
